original_title: quote qop options in Digest Auth
original_body:
quote qop options in Digest Auth
Based on RFC2617 (http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2617), the value of
'qop-options' directive should be quoted with double quotes:

```
qop-options
     This directive is optional, but is made so only for backward
     compatibility with RFC 2069 [6]; it SHOULD be used by all
     implementations compliant with this version of the Digest
     scheme. If present, it is a quoted string of one or more
     tokens indicating the "quality of protection" values supported by
     the server.  The value "auth" indicates authentication; the
     value "auth-int" indicates authentication with
     integrity protection; see the
```

curl comamnd-line tool also appends these quotes. You can see this
by `curl -v --digest --user user:passwd http://example.com/digest-auth`.
Unfortunately, some minor server-side implementations seem to be sensitive
on this difference.
enhanced_title: Fix Digest Auth to properly quote qop options per RFC2617
enhanced_body:
## Summary
The requests library's Digest Authentication implementation doesn't properly quote the qop (quality of protection) options in the Authorization header, which violates RFC2617 specifications.

## Problem Description
According to RFC2617 Section 3.2.1, the qop-options directive in Digest Authentication must be enclosed in double quotes. The current implementation sends unquoted values, which:
1. Violates the RFC specification
2. Causes issues with some strict server implementations
3. Differs from other tools like curl which properly quote these values

## Expected Behavior
The Authorization header should include quoted qop values, for example:
```
Authorization: Digest username=\"user\", realm=\"...\", qop=\"auth,auth-int\", ...
```

## Actual Behavior
The current implementation sends unquoted qop values:
```
Authorization: Digest username=\"user\", realm=\"...\", qop=auth,auth-int, ...
```

## Reproduction Steps
1. Use requests to authenticate with a Digest Auth server
2. Compare the Authorization header with what curl generates:
```bash
curl -v --digest --user user:passwd http://example.com/digest-auth
```
3. Observe that curl properly quotes the qop values while requests does not

## Environment
- Library: requests
- Affected component: Digest Authentication implementation
- Likely affected files: requests/auth.py (based on hints)

## References
- RFC2617 Section 3.2.1
- Similar behavior in curl command-line tool

## Impact
While many servers are lenient with this, some strict implementations may reject the unquoted format, causing authentication failures.

## Proposed Fix
Modify the Digest Authentication implementation to properly quote the qop options in the Authorization header, bringing it into compliance with RFC2617.
